question: What programs are offered at the university?
answer: Engineering (B.Tech with ~18 specializations like CSE, AI&ML, Biotech, Civil), Agriculture (B.Sc Hons), Pharmacy (B.Pharm), Science (M.Sc), Business Administration (BBA/MBA), and Law (BA LLB)

question: What undergraduate programs are available?
answer: B.Tech (4 years) in specializations like CSE, AI&ML, ECE, Biotechnology, and Bioinformatics. Other UG options include B.Pharmacy, B.Sc. (Hons) Agriculture, BBA, BCA, and integrated BA/BBA LLB (Hons).

question: What postgraduate programs are available?
answer: including M.Tech (specializations in CSE, ECE, BioTech, Civil, Mech, Power Electronics), MBA in areas like HR and Finance, MCA, M.Sc in Chemistry/Mathematics, and MA in English. These full-time programs focus on industry relevance, research, and technical educatio

question: What is the eligibility for B.Tech admission?
answer: Admission to B.Tech programs at Vignan (VFSTR) requires a minimum 60% aggregate in 10+2 (Science stream) or equivalent, along with a qualifying rank in V-SAT, JEE Mains, or AP/TS EAMCET. Candidates must have studied Physics, Chemistry, and Mathematics, with specific age and residency requirements also applicable.

question: What is the eligibility for MBA admission?
answer: Eligibility for the 2-year MBA program at Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology & Research (VFSTR) requires a bachelor's degree in any discipline with at least 50%–55% aggregate marks. Candidates must also possess a valid score in management entrance exams like CAT, MAT, XAT, ICET, or CMAT.

question: Which entrance exams are accepted for admission?
answer: Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology & Research (VFSTR) accepts admission primarily through its own entrance test, V-SAT (Vignan's Scholastic Aptitude Test). Other accepted exams include JEE Main, JEE Advanced, and AP EAPCET (formerly EAMCET). For MBA, ICET, MAT (above 500), and CAT are accepted.

question: How can I apply for admission?
answer: To apply for admission to Vignan University (VFSTR), fill out the online application on their official website by creating an account and paying the fees, or submit a physical form via DD. Admission is based on V-SAT, EAMCET, JEE, or other entrance exams, followed by counseling. Applications are open for B.Tech, M.Tech, MBA, and other programs.

question: Where can I fill the admission application form?
answer: You can fill the admission application form for Vignan University online at their official admissions website, admissions.vignan.ac.in. Options include registering for V-SAT or other programs, paying the fee online, and submitting your details directly.

question: What documents are required for admission?
answer: Admission to Vignan University requires key documents including 10th and 12th marks memos, entrance exam rank cards (VSAT/JEE/EAMCET), transfer/migration certificates, and Aadhaar card. Originals and photocopies are required for counseling. For online applications, scanned copies of these documents, along with photos, must be uploaded.

question: What is the admission deadline?
answer: For the 2026 academic year at Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research (VFSTR), the V-SAT entrance exam application deadline is typically February 25, 2026. Exams are scheduled between March 1 and April 15, 2026. However, for specific programs like B.Tech/B.Pharma, registration without late fees can extend to March 17, 2026.

question: Is there an application fee?
answer: Yes, there is an application fee for Vignan University. It is generally INR 1,200 for B.Tech/VSAT and other regular programs, which is non-refundable. For certain postgraduate programs, the application fee may differ, and some specialized or online programs may have different fee structures.

question: Can international students apply?
answer: Yes, international students, including foreign nationals, NRI students, and children of Indians working in the Gulf or Southeast Asia, can apply to Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology & Research (VFSTR),. Applicants must generally have a minimum of 50% aggregate in their qualifying exam. A student visa is required for admission.

question: Is lateral entry available for B.Tech?
answer: Yes, lateral entry is available for B.Tech programs at Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology & Research (Deemed to be University). Eligible diploma or B.Sc. graduates can gain direct entry into the second year (3-year duration). Candidates must pass a diploma with at least 60% aggregate marks, with admission based on VUCET or ECET scores.

question: When will admission results be announced?
answer: VSAT 2026 admission results (Phase I) are expected to be announced a few days after the exam concludes on November 20, 2025, or within two weeks following the last test date in subsequent phases, which run until April 2026. The results are declared online on the Vignan official website, and selected candidates will be called for counseling.

question: Who should I contact for admission queries?
answer: For admission queries at Vignan University (VFSTR), contact the Dean of Admissions at dean_adm@vignan.ac.in or the Admissions Office at +91 863-2344777 / 86. Toll-free support is available at 1800-425-2529, and you can get immediate assistance via WhatsApp at +91 62818 26720 or +91 9608555535 (specifically for Bihar/UP/North regions).

question: Is there any reservation policy?
answer: Yes, Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology & Research (Deemed to be University) (VFSTR) strictly adheres to the reservation policy prescribed by the Government of India and the Andhra Pradesh State Government. The university reserves 49.5% of seats for OBC (27%), SC (15%), and ST (7.5%) categories, along with a 10% quota for Economically Weaker Sections (EWS).

question: Can I change my course after admission?
answer: Yes, you can change your course or branch at Vignan (VFSTR) after admission, but it is strictly subject to vacancy, timing, and academic regulations. This Vignan document states that "sliding" options for changing branches are available within stipulated times, while Vignan Online states that online programs generally do not allow changes after the final deadline.

question: How many credits are required to complete the degree?
answer: B.Tech (4 Years): 160 credits say R22/R23 Regulations, M.Tech (2 Years): 68 credits says Vignan University. BCA (3 Years): 120 credits says Vignan University.M.A. English (2 Years): 80 credits says Vignan University.

question: Where can I find the academic calendar?
answer: You can find the Vignan academic calendar (2025-2026) directly on the official Vignan University website (vignan.ac.in) under the "Academics" or "Exam Cell" sections. It is available for B.Tech, M.Tech, BBA, BCA, and BSc, offering downloadable PDFs for semester schedules.

question: When does the current semester start and end?
answer: Based on the 2025-26 academic calendars for Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research (VFSTR), the academic year is split into two main semesters. While specific dates vary by department, generally, the semester starts with registrations in August and concludes with exams around December-January, followed by a second semester beginning in early 2026.

question: How can I register for courses?
answer: To register for courses at Vignan (VFSTR), students must first clear their tuition fees via the online portal or bank challan, followed by submitting a completed registration form to their department. The process involves: Fee Payment: Registration Form: Counselor Approval: V-SAT (For new admission)

question: Where can I access the course syllabus?
answer: You can access the course syllabus at Vignan University directly through the official website's Academic Regulations page, which offers downloadable PDF options for various programs (B.Tech, M.Tech, MBA, B.Sc). Specific syllabi are listed by regulation (e.g., R22, R25) and department.

question: How can I check my grades or academic results?
answer: To check your grades or academic results at Vignan University, visit the official website's results page (vignan.ac.in/hyd/results.php) or the Examination Management System (ems.vignanlara.org). Select your specific exam/semester link and enter your registered roll number to view results.

question: Who should I contact for academic guidance or advising?
answer: For academic guidance or advising at Vignan (VFSTR), students should first contact their assigned faculty mentor/counselor. For department-specific, curricular, or serious academic issues, contact the Head of the Department (HoD). School Deans are also available for higher-level guidance

question: What is the tuition fee for my program?
answer: Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research (VFSTR) vary by program: B.Tech generally ranges from ₹1,00,000 to ₹3,80,000 per year, depending on specialization and seat category. MBA fees are roughly ₹3.3L to ₹4.1L total, while BBA fees are around ₹1L-₹1.2L annual

question: What is the total fee structure for the academic year?
answer:
B.Tech Tuition Fee: ₹1,00,000 – ₹2,80,000 (Category A) / ₹2,00,000 – ₹3,80,000 (Category B) annually. B.Sc (Hons) Agriculture / B. Pharmacy: ₹1,80,000 (Cat A) - ₹2,80,000 (Cat B) annually.BBA / BCA: ₹1,20,000 (Cat A) - ₹2,20,000 (Cat B) annually.MBA/PGDM: Total fees around ₹4 L - 6.95 L.
Hostel Fees: Approximately ₹4.4 Lakhs total (for 4 years).

question: What payment methods are available for paying fees?
answer: Vignan University offers several, primarily online, methods for paying tuition and other fees. Available methods include online payment through the official website using debit cards, credit cards, net banking, and UPI, or via NEFT/IMPS bank transfers to their Punjab National Bank account

question: What is the deadline for fee payment?
answer: For the II Semester of the 2025-2026 academic year at Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research (VFSTR), the deadline for paying tuition and exam fees is on or before December 22, 2025. Fees should be paid online through the official Vignan website, with registration needed before the commencement of classwork.

question: Are scholarships available for students?
answer: Yes, Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology & Research (Vignan University) offers extensive scholarships worth around ₹16 crores annually, including 75%, 50%, and 25% tuition fee waivers for meritorious students based on V-SAT, JEE Mains, EAMCET, or intermediate marks. These scholarships are available for B.Tech, B.Pharm, B.Sc, M.Tech, and other programs.

question: What are the eligibility criteria for scholarships?
answer: Scholarships at Vignan (Deemed to be University) are primarily awarded based on merit, including performance in V-SAT, JEE Mains/Advanced, or EAMCET, typically requiring ranks below 40,000 for Engineering. To maintain scholarships, students must maintain 70%+ marks without backlogs and exhibit good discipline.

question: Does the university provide education loan assistance?
answer: Yes, Vignan (VFSTR) provides education loan assistance by facilitating partnerships with over 14 public and private banks to provide customized loan solutions. The on-campus UCO Bank branch also helps facilitate these services for tuition and other expenses.

question: What is the refund policy for tuition fees?
answer: Student must get atleast 70% marks in their previous semister.

question: Are there any penalties for late fee payment?
answer: No there will not be any.

question: Who should I contact for fee-related queries?
answer: For fee-related queries at Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology & Research (VFSTR), you should contact the Finance Department or the following officials:

question: What hostel facilities are available for students?
answer: Vignan (VFSTR) offers on-campus, secure, and separate hostel facilities for boys and girls, accommodating over 1,500 boys and 1,300 girls. Amenities include A/C and non-A/C rooms (single to four-bed), nutritious mess food, Wi-Fi, 24/7 security with CCTV, a medical dispensary, a gym, and laundry services.

question: What are the hostel room types available?
answer: Vignan University offers on-campus, separate hostels for boys and girls with varied room types, including AC, AC Deluxe, and Non-AC options, accommodating 4 to 7 students per room depending on the building. Rooms are furnished with beds, study tables, chairs, and cupboards, with choices for shared (double, triple, or four-occupancy) setup

question: What are the hostel rules and regulations?
answer: Vignan University hostel regulations emphasize strict discipline, banning ragging, alcohol, and drugs, with a 10:00 PM return deadline for students leaving after 6:00 PM. Smoking and intoxicating substances are prohibited, leading to immediate dismissal. Students must follow warden instructions, keep rooms clean, and avoid unauthorized electrical appliances.

question: Is Wi-Fi available in the hostel and campus?
answer: Yes, Wi-Fi is available 24/7 across the entire campus and inside the hostels at Vignan (VFSTR), Guntur. The campus features high-speed internet (1 Gbps) with Wi-Fi access in academic blocks, classrooms, and residential areas to facilitate learning.

question: What are the library timings?
answer: The NTR Library at Vignan University is open daily from 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM to serve students and staff. Book issue and return services are available from 9:00 AM to 6:00 PM on working days. The library provides access to a wide range of academic resources, journals, and a dedicated digital section

question: How can I access the digital library resources?
answer: Access Vignan University's digital library resources, including 6,748+ e-journals (IEEE, ASME, ASCE, Springer, JSTOR) and 200,000+ e-books, primarily via campus IP-based access or by obtaining a username and password from the library for remote access. Key resources are available through the VignanLibrary app on Google Play or the online IDP porta

question: Are computer labs available for students?
answer: Yes, computer labs are readily available for students at Vignan University (VFSTR), offering modern, state-of-the-art facilities across various departments, including dedicated labs for programming, robotics, and simulations. These labs are equipped with the latest software, high-speed internet, and supporting staff to facilitate hands-on learning.

question: What sports facilities are available on campus?
answer: Vignan (VFSTR) offers extensive, state-of-the-art sports facilities for both indoor and outdoor games, managed by a team of Physical Directors and coaches. Key amenities include a 400-meter athletic track, cricket ground with practice nets, basketball, football, volleyball, kabaddi, and tennis courts. Indoor facilities feature table tennis, chess, and badminton

question: Is there a gym or fitness center on campus?
answer: Yes, Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research (VFSTR) in Guntur has on-campus gym facilities, often equipped with modern machines to support student fitness. The university focuses on holistic health, featuring both indoor/outdoor sports, a swimming pool, and dedicated physical directors/coaches,

question: Is transportation available for students?
answer: Yes, extensive transportation is available for students at Vignan. Vignan University operates a fleet of 40-60+ buses covering Guntur, Vijayawada, Tenali, Mangalagiri, and surrounding areas within a 45-50 km radius. The services are designed for safety, with cameras installed, and provide a capacity for over 2,500 students.

question: What are the university bus routes and timings?
answer: Vignan University operates a fleet of over 40-60 buses covering Guntur, Vijayawada, Tenali, Mangalagiri, and surrounding areas (up to 45-50 km radius) to transport staff and students to the Vadlamudi campus. Buses generally run in the morning (around 7:30-8:00 AM) to the campus and return after 5:00 PM, with specific route timings provided by the transport office

question: Is there a cafeteria or mess facility on campus?
answer: Yes, Vignan University (VFSTR) has comprehensive on-campus dining facilities, including a cafeteria, multiple food kiosks, and a mess facility serving breakfast, lunch, and dinner to students. The campus features spacious dining halls that offer vegetarian, nutritious, and hygienic meals, along with specialized self-kitchen facilities for students.

question: Where can I find the campus map?
answer: A detailed campus map for Vignan's Foundation for Science, Technology and Research (VFSTR) in Vadlamudi, Guntur, can be found directly on the official Vignan University website. It is located along the Guntur-Tenali highway and is easily accessible via the Vignan University campus, which spans over 40 acres

question: Are medical or health services available on campus?
answer: Yes, comprehensive medical and health services are available on the Vignan (VFSTR) campus in Vadlamudi, Guntur. The university prioritizes student well-being with several on-campus facilities: Health Care Center, Hostel Dispensary, 24/7 Emergency Services, Medical Support & Insurance.

question: Who should I contact for hostel or facility-related issues?
answer: For hostel or facility-related issues at Vignan (VFSTR), you should contact the Dean of Student Affairs (Dean, SA), who oversees hostel management. For immediate, specific maintenance issues, you can file a complaint through the online Grievance Redressal system or contact the hostel warden/office of Dean SA.
